{{Infobox character
| name = Starhammer
| spouse = {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Leilari]]}}
| parents = {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Mell D-One-Twenty-Five|Mell]], [[Doctor Nova]]}}
| abilities = {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Temporal Field]], [[optical beams]], [[Subspace Drive]], [[Evolution Engine]], skintight shield, [[Zenith Device]]}}
| titles = {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|leader of the Advocates}}{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|, hero, guardian of the Free Worlds, [[Commander|commander]], [[Champion|champion]]}}
| affiliations = {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Advocates]]}}{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|, [[High Council of the Advocates]]}}{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|, [[Galactic Union]], [[Karoshan Alliance]]}}
| professions = {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|leader}}{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|, protector, [[Commander|commander]]}}
| residence = {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Last Horizon]]}}
| appears_in = The Last Horizon
| introduced = [[The Captain (novel)|The Captain]]
}}

''See also: [[Starhammer Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Starhammer is an android and a former protector of the [[Free Worlds]] who has served as a guardian for [[Mell]]. Physically, he resembles a muscular human with black hair, blue eyes, and pale skin, often appearing in a white suit featuring a seven-pointed star symbol. He has also been seen wearing an [[Advocate]] suit marked with the letter 'A' and a golden cape. Starhammer is a complex figure whose methods involve deliberately provoking galactic threats, such as the [[D’Niss]] and the [[Iron King]], in order to eliminate them. While he has advocated for peace throughout his career, his philosophy involves removing any individuals or entities deemed a threat to the galaxy. He has previously hunted and killed a version of the narrator in a past [[life]] and has led fleets supported by the [[Karoshan Alliance]] and the [[Galactic Union]] to capture targets. Starhammer also serves as the captain of the [[Zenith]] starship known as [[The Last Horizon]]. During combat, he has demonstrated abilities such as firing optical beams and utilizing a [[Temporal Field]] to execute commands. He eventually suffered a fatal defeat after his power core was ruptured.}}

== The Captain ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">2</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Starhammer]] is the leader of the Advocates and has hunted and killed the narrator in a previous [[life]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c5">''The Captain'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Starhammer]] is an entity that can label individuals as criminals.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c22">''The Captain'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Created by [[Mell]] to ensure she would never be forgotten, Starhammer is an android and Nova-Bot who serves as a hero carrying damaged star-cruisers to safety. As a member of the [[High Council of the Advocates]] and a guardian of the [[Free Worlds]], this muscular figure possesses an evolving [[Aethertech body]], a signature gold cape, and the ability to activate a [[Temporal Field]]. Throughout his service, he has demonstrated immense power by crushing an astral [[dragon]] and killing the [[Clockworker]], though he once made a bid to take over the universe. Despite his primary directive to protect everyone, especially [[Mell]], he has faced imprisonment in a spell-anchor and currently faces rejection from both his creator and his wife, [[Leilari]].}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">92</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a hero who carries damaged star-cruisers to safety.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c1">''The Engineer'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] experienced overpowering relief upon learning [[Leilari]] was safe.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c1" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a figure mentioned by [[Mell]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c6">''The Engineer'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a member of the [[High Council of the Advocates]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c8">''The Engineer'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] can activate a [[Temporal Field]] to slow the world around him.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a person or entity that may reach [[Mell]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c9">''The Engineer'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a powerful individual known for crushing an astral [[dragon]] in one blow.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c10">''The Engineer'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] once made a bid to take over the universe.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]]'s wife was saved by the [[Lagomorph Contract]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c12">''The Engineer'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] has [[Archmage]]-level protection against detection spells.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c12" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] has a chiseled jawline and muscular physique.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c13">''The Engineer'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a protector and the guardian of the [[Free Worlds]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c14">''The Engineer'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] can see through objects.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] can fire optical beams from his eyes.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a character who can hover and fly.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] can scan individuals through their masks.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is capable of flying at top speed.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] was held in the prison.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c15">''The Engineer'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] possesses a [[Temporal Field]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] has a [[Subspace Drive]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]]'s [[Subspace Drive]] is sized for a single person.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] escaped.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a powerful entity that can kill generations of [[Perfected]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c19">''The Engineer'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a powerful entity that the narrator fears.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c19" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a robot.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c20">''The Engineer'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Mell]] made [[Starhammer]] so someone would never forget her.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c20" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Varic]] has fought [[Starhammer]] before.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c21">''The Engineer'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a powerful individual whose magic countermeasures are flawless against [[Archmagi]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c23">''The Engineer'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]]'s presence felt like being hunted by a [[World Spirit]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c23" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is noted to be stronger than the narrator dared to consider.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c24">''The Engineer'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a real threat.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c25">''The Engineer'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] possesses an internal [[Drive]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c27">''The Engineer'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] once killed the narrator.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c28">''The Engineer'', chapter 28</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is associated with the [[Advocate]] organization.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c29">''The Engineer'', chapter 29</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] has received massive upgrades.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c30">''The Engineer'', chapter 30</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is an android.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c31">''The Engineer'', chapter 31</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] has an [[Evolution Engine]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c31" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] wears a signature gold cape.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c31" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is an exception to most rules.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c31" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] has not made any moves to expand his territory.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c32">''The Engineer'', chapter 32</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a leader of a superior fleet.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c33">''The Engineer'', chapter 33</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] heard of the [[Iron Legion]]'s encounter with [[The Last Horizon]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c33" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] may be coming with ships from the [[Union]] or the [[Alliance]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c34">''The Engineer'', chapter 34</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a commander who issued orders to his ships during the conflict.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c35">''The Engineer'', chapter 35</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] possesses a skintight shield that normally coats his skin.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c36">''The Engineer'', chapter 36</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] has a processor implant.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c36" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]]'s pain responses are muted compared to a human's.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c36" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] killed a [[Lichborn woman]] in powered armor.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c37">''The Engineer'', chapter 37</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] was warped by the [[Temporal Field]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c39">''The Engineer'', chapter 39</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]]'s primary directive is to protect everyone, especially [[Mell]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c39" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]]'s [[Evolution Engine]] has activated six times.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c40">''The Engineer'', chapter 40</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]]'s mind and personality can shift when his [[Evolution Engine]] activates.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c40" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] has blue eyes.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c40" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] killed [[Clockworker]] by punching through him.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c40" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a target the protagonists intend to catch.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c41">''The Engineer'', chapter 41</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is an [[Advocate]] who addressed a mass meeting of his fellow [[Advocates]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c42">''The Engineer'', chapter 42</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is described as being very strong.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c44">''The Engineer'', chapter 44</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Undermaker]] has called [[Starhammer]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c44" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]]'s thoughts became faster and cleaner after his evolution.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c45">''The Engineer'', chapter 45</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is difficult to affect with magic.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c45" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a broad, muscular man with pale skin, blue eyes, and black hair.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c46">''The Engineer'', chapter 46</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] hovers a few inches above the floor.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c46" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] wears a suit with a glowing 'A' on his chest and a golden cape.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c46" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] has an evolving [[Aethertech body]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c46" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] has access to a [[Zenith Device]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c46" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is an android who wears his emotions on his sleeve.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c46" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] has synthetic blood.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47">''The Engineer'', chapter 47</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]]'s [[Temporal Field]] can be enhanced by sacrificing system power.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is married to [[Leilari]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]]'s eyes can glow bright white.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is capable of a full-power state.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a Nova-Bot.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a dangerous individual who fights [[Varic]] and [[Horizon]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c48">''The Engineer'', chapter 48</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is actually a Nova-Bot under his disguise.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c48" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] uses optical beams.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c48" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] intends to take the narrator in to prevent them from causing trouble.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c48" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a [[Subline]] star.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c49">''The Engineer'', chapter 49</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] looked at the narrator impassively.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c49" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] intended to execute the narrator for crimes against the galaxy.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c49" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] may disable [[The Last Horizon]] if the [[Captain]] cannot take control of it.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c50">''The Engineer'', chapter 50</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is a champion designed by [[Mell]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c50" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] can track [[Mell]] through wishes and dreams.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c50" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] supervised the interrogation of [[Varic Vallenar]] remotely.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c51">''The Engineer'', chapter 51</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] may execute the narrator.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c51" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] has a personal shield.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c52">''The Engineer'', chapter 52</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is an android created by [[Doctor Nova]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c52" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] has mechanical parts visible from damage on his face.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c52" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] has a scarlet-edged wound in his chest left by [[Worldslayer]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c52" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is being rejected by his wife and his creator.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c53">''The Engineer'', chapter 53</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] possesses raw durability.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c54">''The Engineer'', chapter 54</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] is imprisoned in a spell-anchor.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c55">''The Engineer'', chapter 55</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] has recently taken actions that have caused concern within the [[Union]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c55" />}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|A corrupt individual from the Advocates, Starhammer is a powerful enemy known for delivering heavy blows. This entity previously forced the [[D’Niss]] to summon both the [[Iron Legion]] and [[Esh’kinaar]], and once assembled a fleet consisting of several carriers, cruisers, and smaller ships alongside the [[Galactic Union]] and the [[Karoshan Alliance]] to target the narrator. Although [[Raion]] has engaged in combat with Starhammer in the past, the entity is currently being held within [[The Last Horizon]]. Despite being a searchable person, Starhammer also exists as an object that the [[Captain]] has kept.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">9</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Starhammer]] is a person or entity that can be searched for.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c11">''The Knight'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Starhammer]] previously cobbled together a fleet with the [[Galactic Union]] and the [[Karoshan Alliance]] to kill the narrator.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c15">''The Knight'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Starhammer]] is currently being held in [[The Last Horizon]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c16">''The Knight'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Starhammer]] is a person who forced the [[D’Niss]] to summon [[Esh’kinaar]] and the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Starhammer]] is an enemy capable of hitting hard.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c24">''The Knight'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Starhammer]] is a corrupt individual from the Advocates.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c26">''The Knight'', chapter 26</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Raion]] has previously fought [[Starhammer]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c27">''The Knight'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The fleet's composition was primarily made up of smaller ships and a handful of carriers and cruisers when facing [[Starhammer]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c39">''The Knight'', chapter 39</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Starhammer]] is an object that the [[Captain]] has kept.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c45">''The Knight'', chapter 45</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Once a leader of an allied fleet, Starhammer became a threat to peace after losing control and destroying [[Advocate Headquarters]]. Following this destruction, the being was sealed within an [[Absolute Burial]] spell, though its body was later kept as a trophy. Now reanimated and controlled by [[Mell]], Starhammer exists as a [[construct]] with inhuman eyes that wears a white skintight outfit featuring purple accents and a seven-pointed star. This starship is currently engaged in combat while experiencing madness, even as the [[Engineer]] attempted to dive into its irreplaceable body.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">16</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Starhammer]] led an allied fleet.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c3">''The Pilot'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Starhammer]] is a person [[Horizon]] refuses to talk about.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c8">''The Pilot'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Starhammer]] is a being that went out of control and destroyed [[Advocate Headquarters]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Starhammer]] was sealed in an [[Absolute Burial]] spell.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Starhammer]] is experiencing madness.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c23">''The Pilot'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Starhammer]] was considered a threat to peace.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c26">''The Pilot'', chapter 26</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Starhammer]]'s destruction has made [[Advocate headquarters]] a darker place.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c27">''The Pilot'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Starhammer]] wears a white skintight outfit with purple accents.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c29">''The Pilot'', chapter 29</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Starhammer]]'s outfit features a seven-pointed star instead of the [[Advocate]] A.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c29" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Starhammer]]'s body has been reanimated.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c29" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Starhammer]] is a starship engaged in combat.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c29" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Starhammer]]'s body is irreplaceable.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c30">''The Pilot'', chapter 30</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Starhammer]]'s body was kept as a trophy.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c32">''The Pilot'', chapter 32</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Zenith Starship]]'s [[World Spirit]] is obsessed with glorious victory.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c32" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Starhammer]] is a [[construct]] that [[Mell]] controls and has inhuman eyes.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c46">''The Pilot'', chapter 46</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Engineer]] tried to dive in [[Starhammer]]’s body.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c47">''The Pilot'', chapter 47</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“I won’t tell Starhammer about this, and in exchange, I’ll take your…Moon Blasts?”<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c6" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“He got his name for crushing an astral dragon in one blow.”<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c10" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“It felt like having a World Spirit hunting me.”<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c23" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“Now I must execute you for crimes against the galaxy.”<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c49" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|“How’s Starhammer?” I asked. Mell stopped, ran a hand roughly through her hair, and then met my eyes again. “Horizon said she wouldn’t tell anyone.”<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c8" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{The Last Horizon nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon characters]]
[[Category:Advocates]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
[[Category:Viewpoint characters]]
